title: Admitted Students
description: Congratulations on your offer of admission to NYU Shanghai's Class of 2030!We are thrilled to welcome you to the NYU Shanghai family. Each year, NYU’s applicant pool becomes larger and more competitive, and you should be very proud of your accomplishment. On this page, you’ll find important
text:
Admitted Students
Congratulations on your offer of admission to NYU Shanghai's Class of 2030!
We are thrilled to welcome you to the NYU Shanghai family. Each year, NYU’s applicant pool becomes larger and more competitive, and you should be very proud of your accomplishment. On this page, you’ll find important information about the steps you need to take to confirm your enrollment at NYU Shanghai. You'll also learn about our admitted student activities and how to complete other relevant items so you're all set by the first day of classes.
Admitted Student Portal
Access your Home Base to stay on track, stay hyped, and find important information about student life, housing, academics, and more. Use your NYU NetID and password to log in. *Look for an email titled "Your NYU ID Numbers and Status Page" if you don't remember it.
First thing’s first: Let’s make this thing official.
Go to NYUAlbert and click on “View Application Status.” Click on "Admitted Student's Portal." Then click "Accept/Decline" and submit your non-refundable enrollment deposit by credit card or electronic check for US citizens or permanent residents and by wire transfer for non-US citizens to reserve your place in the class.
Deadlines to accept our offer and join NYU Shanghai Class of 2030:
Early Decision I: January 10
Early Decision II: March 1
Regular Decision: May 1
Please respond to your offer of admission by the deadlines stated above. After the deadline passes, we may relinquish your place in the class. Keep in mind, if you accept our offer, you must withdraw any applications you have pending at other colleges or universities.
If you have any issues submitting your payment, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions at shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu.
If you applied for financial aid by the NYU financial aid application deadline and received a financial aid package, you can view your offer on your admitted student portal.
NYU is committed to helping you navigate the financial aid process. Learn more about how to accept your financial aid, payment plans, loan options, and financial aid renewal.
NYU will send critical information about the enrollment process to your NYU email address. Soon after you confirm your enrollment, you’ll be assigned an NYU email address and given instructions on 1) how to create your account and 2) set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) to help protect your sensitive information online. Once created, please check your email regularly. Contact NYU’s global IT Service Desk at AskITS@nyu.edu if you have trouble creating your account.
Class of 2030 Admitted Students
We are excited to offer a variety of events for our Regular Decision admits—both in-person and virtual—to help you connect with our community and get answers to your questions. These events will also give you a chance to meet other admitted students and allow your parents to engage with our team. For events that require registration, you can sign up through your Home Base (NYU admitted student website).
Parents as Partners: Supporting Your Student’s Journey at NYU Shanghai
Tuesday, March 31, 2026, 9:00 PM (ET)
Join David Pe, Ed.D., Assistant Vice Chancellor for Student Affairs and Dean of Students, for an engaging virtual session designed to help parents of admitted students feel confident and excited about their student’s decision to join NYU Shanghai.
Discover how NYU Shanghai’s world-class academics, global opportunities, and comprehensive support systems prepare students to thrive. Gain insights into resources available, strategies for a smooth transition, and answers to your questions.
Advanced registration is required via Home Base.
Weekend on the Square: NYU Shanghai Session (New York Campus)
Saturday, April 11, 2026, 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM (ET)
Join us during Weekend on the Square for a special NYU Shanghai session designed for admitted students and families. Learn more about our academic experience, student life, and global opportunities, and connect with the community that may shape your next four years.
The session will take place at the Kimmel Center for University Life (Rooms 905/907), followed by a lunch mingler from 1:00 – 1:45 PM.
Advanced registration is required via Home Base.
Life in Shanghai: For Students, by Students
Monday, April 13, 2026, 9:00 PM (ET)
Curious what students do around Shanghai when they’re not studying? Join this virtual session to hear directly from current NYU Shanghai students who were once in your shoes. They’ll share their favorite places to explore and offer tips for making the most of life in this vibrant 26-million–person city full of culture, neighborhoods, and experiences beyond the classroom.
Advanced registration is required via Home Base.
Admitted Student Weekend
April 24–25, 2026
We are excited to welcome you to Shanghai for Admitted Student Weekend, April 24–25, 2026. This immersive program offers a firsthand look at life at NYU Shanghai and the opportunity to begin envisioning your future here. You will tour campus and the residence halls, learn about academic and student life resources, connect with current students, future classmates, faculty, and university leadership, and explore Shanghai and Chinese culture.
This weekend marks the beginning of your NYU Shanghai journey and a chance to start feeling at home in our community. We hope you will join us!
Click here for more information and sign up via Home Base by April 5, 2026.
Instagram Takeover
April 1–3, 2026
April 9, 16, and 23, 2026 (Thursdays)
Join us for a series of Instagram takeovers, where current students will share glimpses of their daily lives and answer your questions about what it’s like to live and study in one of the world’s most dynamic cities! Follow @nyushanghai on Instagram to catch the takeovers.
Coffee Chats with Admissions Counselors & Current Students
Available through April 30, 2026
Our community is excited to meet you and answer any questions you have about NYU Shanghai! Whether you’re curious about academics, campus life, or adjusting to life in Shanghai, you can register through Home Base to schedule a one-on-one virtual chat with a current student or an admissions representative.
NYU Shanghai requires all admitted students to submit final transcripts and proof of high school graduation from all institutions attended. These documents must be received by NYU’s Office of Undergraduate Admissions by July 15, 2026. Failure to meet this deadline will result in the withdrawal of your enrollment. Submitting these materials is a required final step before enrolling and traveling to China on your student visa.
For full details, please review NYU’s Proof of Graduation Policy.
If your school operates on a different academic calendar and cannot meet the deadline, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions at shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu to request an extension.
Submission Instructions:
Preferred: Have your final transcript submitted electronically directly to NYU.
Preferred: Have your final transcript submitted electronically directly to NYU.
By Mail (if necessary): If your school is unable to submit electronically, transcripts must be mailed to:New York UniversityOffice of Undergraduate Admissions400 Lafayette StreetNew York, NY 10003 USANotice: Please do not mail any documents to NYU Shanghai’s campus in China.
For Students from Non-U.S. High Schools:
Students graduating from schools outside the U.S. must submit their final transcripts and proof of graduation to Educational Credential Evaluators (ECE) for authentication. Once verified, ECE will share the results with NYU Shanghai. You can find instructions for sending these final documents to ECE here.
If your documents are released after the deadline, submit them to ECE as soon as possible. While your fall enrollment will not be affected, missing documents may delay your ability to register for future semesters.
Please ensure:
Your name on all documents matches your Common Application exactly.
Your date of birth is included on all materials sent to ECE.
Note: Official test scores (e.g., A-levels from Cambridge International Examinations and the IB) submitted through official electronic sources do not need to be sent to ECE. If you received an IB diploma, please request that a copy of your official transcript be sent from the IBO directly to NYU.
If you have any questions, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions at shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu.
Now that you've received your offer of admission it's likely that you have questions, want to meet current students, and/or talk with staff members from across the University. Check out the ways to connect with us below:
Chat 1-on-1 with Admissions and/or Current Students
Interested in talking with a current NYU Shanghai student about their experience? Have questions for one of our admissions representatives? Sign up for a 1-on-1 virtual chat by filling out the form on your Home Base. Someone from the NYU Shanghai community will be in touch with you in the coming weeks to set up a time to connect. Families are invited to join this conversation!
Class of 2030 Facebook Group
If you haven’t already, make sure to join our NYU Shanghai Class of 2030 Facebook group to get to know your fellow classmates, learn everything you need to know about NYU Shanghai, and stay up-to-date with important information. Our current students will also host activities in the group so you can start meeting your future classmates! You can also follow NYU Shanghai on our main Facebook, Weibo, Instagram,YouTube, and X channels.
Financial Aid
If you applied for financial aid by the NYU financial aid application deadline and received a financial aid package, you can view your offer on your admitted student portal. Please keep in mind that NYU Shanghai offers the best package in the initial financial aid award.
If you have any questions about financial aid, please contact the Office of Financial Support directly by emailing shanghai.financial.support@nyu.edu or by calling +1-212-998-2838 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m., Eastern Standard Time).
Resources for Parents
We'd never forget about the parents and guardians that have helped their students reach this exceptional milestone. Join our NYU Shanghai Parents and Families Facebook group designed exclusively for parents, guardians, and family members of NYU Shanghai students. This is a great way for you to connect with other members of our community, as well as get your questions answered by parents of current NYU Shanghai students.
If you have additional questions outside of what you'd like to discuss in the Facebook group, you can always reach out to shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu. Additionally, you can find out more information for NYU Shanghai parents on our website.
Confirmed your seat and submitted your enrollment deposit - What's next?
We know you're eager to pack your bags and move to China!
Make sure to complete the steps above and continue to engage with your future classmates in our NYU Shanghai Class of 2030 Facebook group.
Starting in mid-May, the Center for New Student Programs will reach out to enrolled students to initiate the visa process (please ensure you have a legal and valid passport to complete all requirements). Over the summer, they will also share information about class registration, the housing preferences application, the Chinese placement exam, bill payments, immunization forms, and more. Students will receive detailed instructions for the required move-in on Sunday, August 23, 2026, as well as guidance for the mandatory orientation program from Monday, August 25 through Saturday, August 30.
Update Chosen/Preferred Name and Pronouns​
You can update your chosen/preferred name and pronouns in Albert (NYU's student information system) at any time. Access instructions for updating your chosen/preferred name and instructions for updating your pronouns at the provided links.
Students living outside of China will receive guidance from the NYU Shanghai Student Mobility team on the student visa application process beginning in May. You will be asked to complete a mandatory visa survey by June 1, and students must secure their student visa by July 1. Failure to complete the survey, obtain a visa by July 1, or secure a residence permit upon arrival (if required) will make you ineligible to study at NYU Shanghai.
Students should also check now to ensure they have a passport valid at least until August 2027. If not, they should apply for a new passport or begin the renewal process as soon as possible, as a valid passport is required for the visa process.
If you have any questions about your visa or residence permit status, please contact the Student Mobility office directly at shanghai.student-mobility@nyu.edu.
You’re not alone as you make the transition from high school to NYU Shanghai. Below, you’ll find contact information for various offices throughout the university. If you need assistance, and you can’t find what you’re looking for on the NYU Shanghai website, please do not hesitate to contact the appropriate office.
Admissions Office in New York (9am–5pm, Eastern Standard Time)+1-212-998-4500 shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu
Admissions Office in Shanghai (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5599shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu
Financial Aid (9am–5pm, Eastern Standard Time)+1-212-998-2838shanghai.financial.support@nyu.edu
Student Health Center (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5353shanghai.health@nyu.edu
Information Technology (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5555shanghai.it.help@nyu.edu
Student Mobility (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5339shanghai.student-mobility@nyu.edu

Congratulations on your offer of admission to NYU Shanghai's Class of 2030!

We are thrilled to welcome you to the NYU Shanghai family. Each year, NYU’s applicant pool becomes larger and more competitive, and you should be very proud of your accomplishment. On this page, you’ll find important information about the steps you need to take to confirm your enrollment at NYU Shanghai. You'll also learn about our admitted student activities and how to complete other relevant items so you're all set by the first day of classes.

Deadlines to accept our offer and join NYU Shanghai Class of 2030:

Early Decision II: March 1
Regular Decision: May 1

Please respond to your offer of admission by the deadlines stated above. After the deadline passes, we may relinquish your place in the class. Keep in mind, if you accept our offer, you must withdraw any applications you have pending at other colleges or universities.
If you have any issues submitting your payment, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions at shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu.

NYU is committed to helping you navigate the financial aid process. Learn more about how to accept your financial aid, payment plans, loan options, and financial aid renewal.

Soon after you confirm your enrollment, you’ll be assigned an NYU email address and given instructions on 1) how to create your account and 2) set up Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) to help protect your sensitive information online. Once created, please check your email regularly. Contact NYU’s global IT Service Desk at AskITS@nyu.edu if you have trouble creating your account.

We are excited to offer a variety of events for our Regular Decision admits—both in-person and virtual—to help you connect with our community and get answers to your questions. Our community is excited to meet you and answer any questions you have about NYU Shanghai! Whether you’re curious about academics, campus life, or adjusting to life in Shanghai, you can register through Home Base to schedule a one-on-one virtual chat with a current student or an admissions representative.

NYU Shanghai requires all admitted students to submit final transcripts and proof of high school graduation from all institutions attended. These documents must be received by NYU’s Office of Undergraduate Admissions by July 15, 2026. Failure to meet this deadline will result in the withdrawal of your enrollment. Submitting these materials is a required final step before enrolling and traveling to China on your student visa.

If your school operates on a different academic calendar and cannot meet the deadline, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions at shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu to request an extension.
Submission Instructions:
Preferred: Have your final transcript submitted electronically directly to NYU.
Preferred: Have your final transcript submitted electronically directly to NYU.

By Mail (if necessary): If your school is unable to submit electronically, transcripts must be mailed to:New York UniversityOffice of Undergraduate Admissions400 Lafayette StreetNew York, NY 10003 USANotice: Please do not mail any documents to NYU Shanghai’s campus in China.
For Students from Non-U.S. High Schools:

Students graduating from schools outside the U.S. must submit their final transcripts and proof of graduation to Educational Credential Evaluators (ECE) for authentication. Once verified, ECE will share the results with NYU Shanghai. You can find instructions for sending these final documents to ECE here.

If your documents are released after the deadline, submit them to ECE as soon as possible. While your fall enrollment will not be affected, missing documents may delay your ability to register for future semesters.
Please ensure:
Your name on all documents matches your Common Application exactly.
Your date of birth is included on all materials sent to ECE.

Note: Official test scores (e.g., A-levels from Cambridge International Examinations and the IB) submitted through official electronic sources do not need to be sent to ECE. If you received an IB diploma, please request that a copy of your official transcript be sent from the IBO directly to NYU.
If you have any questions, please contact the Office of Undergraduate Admissions at shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu.

Now that you've received your offer of admission it's likely that you have questions, want to meet current students, and/or talk with staff members from across the University. Check out the ways to connect with us below:
Chat 1-on-1 with Admissions and/or Current Students

Interested in talking with a current NYU Shanghai student about their experience? Have questions for one of our admissions representatives? Sign up for a 1-on-1 virtual chat by filling out the form on your Home Base. Someone from the NYU Shanghai community will be in touch with you in the coming weeks to set up a time to connect. Families are invited to join this conversation!
Class of 2030 Facebook Group

If you haven’t already, make sure to join our NYU Shanghai Class of 2030 Facebook group to get to know your fellow classmates, learn everything you need to know about NYU Shanghai, and stay up-to-date with important information. Our current students will also host activities in the group so you can start meeting your future classmates! You can also follow NYU Shanghai on our main Facebook, Weibo, Instagram,YouTube, and X channels.
Financial Aid

If you applied for financial aid by the NYU financial aid application deadline and received a financial aid package, you can view your offer on your admitted student portal. Please keep in mind that NYU Shanghai offers the best package in the initial financial aid award.

If you have any questions about financial aid, please contact the Office of Financial Support directly by emailing shanghai.financial.support@nyu.edu or by calling +1-212-998-2838 (9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m., Eastern Standard Time).
Resources for Parents

We'd never forget about the parents and guardians that have helped their students reach this exceptional milestone. Join our NYU Shanghai Parents and Families Facebook group designed exclusively for parents, guardians, and family members of NYU Shanghai students. This is a great way for you to connect with other members of our community, as well as get your questions answered by parents of current NYU Shanghai students.

If you have additional questions outside of what you'd like to discuss in the Facebook group, you can always reach out to shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu. Additionally, you can find out more information for NYU Shanghai parents on our website.
Confirmed your seat and submitted your enrollment deposit - What's next?
We know you're eager to pack your bags and move to China!
Make sure to complete the steps above and continue to engage with your future classmates in our NYU Shanghai Class of 2030 Facebook group.

Starting in mid-May, the Center for New Student Programs will reach out to enrolled students to initiate the visa process (please ensure you have a legal and valid passport to complete all requirements). Over the summer, they will also share information about class registration, the housing preferences application, the Chinese placement exam, bill payments, immunization forms, and more. Students will receive detailed instructions for the required move-in on Sunday, August 23, 2026, as well as guidance for the mandatory orientation program from Monday, August 25 through Saturday, August 30.
Update Chosen/Preferred Name and Pronouns​

You can update your chosen/preferred name and pronouns in Albert (NYU's student information system) at any time. Access instructions for updating your chosen/preferred name and instructions for updating your pronouns at the provided links.

Students living outside of China will receive guidance from the NYU Shanghai Student Mobility team on the student visa application process beginning in May. You will be asked to complete a mandatory visa survey by June 1, and students must secure their student visa by July 1. Failure to complete the survey, obtain a visa by July 1, or secure a residence permit upon arrival (if required) will make you ineligible to study at NYU Shanghai.

Students should also check now to ensure they have a passport valid at least until August 2027. If not, they should apply for a new passport or begin the renewal process as soon as possible, as a valid passport is required for the visa process.
If you have any questions about your visa or residence permit status, please contact the Student Mobility office directly at shanghai.student-mobility@nyu.edu.

You’re not alone as you make the transition from high school to NYU Shanghai. Below, you’ll find contact information for various offices throughout the university. If you need assistance, and you can’t find what you’re looking for on the NYU Shanghai website, please do not hesitate to contact the appropriate office.
Admissions Office in New York (9am–5pm, Eastern Standard Time)+1-212-998-4500 shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu
Admissions Office in Shanghai (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5599shanghai.admissions@nyu.edu
Financial Aid (9am–5pm, Eastern Standard Time)+1-212-998-2838shanghai.financial.support@nyu.edu
Student Health Center (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5353shanghai.health@nyu.edu

Student Mobility (9am–5pm, China Standard Time)+86-21-2059-5339shanghai.student-mobility@nyu.edu
